current_conditions.site_name: ETOWAH RIVER AT GA 9, NEAR DAWSONVILLE, GA

water_temp_read.read: Water is very warm for trout.

light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

caution_notes: Warm water may increase trout stress.

generated_text.quick_read: Trout fishing on the Etowah River is tough due to very warm water temperatures near 77°F. Recent stocking improves chances with eggs and attractor nymphs despite challenging conditions.
generated_text.best_window: The best fishing window is day 2 morning because cooler morning air temperatures (around 71°F), lower rain probability, and light sky cover create the most comfortable conditions for trout to feed near cold-water refuges and oxygenated pockets.
generated_text.current_conditions: Water temperature is very warm at 76.6°F, flow is 105 cfs, gage height is 3.75 ft, and turbidity is moderate at 11.3 NTU.
generated_text.weather_setup: Sky cover averages 23% with mild west winds around 4 mph and gusts to 12.7 mph. Pressure is steady at 30.0 inHg with no rain expected currently, though evening rain probability ranges up to 53%.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout are likely holding near cold-water refuges, oxygenated water, runs, seams, and structure. Present small natural patterns and stocked trout attractors with clean drifts. Use egg patterns, squirmy worms, and duracell flies due to recent stocking. Warm water stresses fish, so consider not targeting trout aggressively and focus on a balanced nymph approach.
